briefdescription.para: Storage container for the characters.
Immutable forward iterator.
Immutable reverse iterator.
Unsigned size type.
Signed distance type.
Stored value type.
Immutable element reference.
Immutable element pointer.
No valid position.
Shared backing storage.
Visible sub-range inside _data.
Create an empty string view.
Create a string view from a string.

detaileddescription.para:
This conversion is implicit so APIs can migrate from String to StringView.
string
The source string.

briefdescription.para: Access one character without bounds checking.

simplesect.para: A copy of the character at index, or Char{} if index is out of bounds.

briefdescription.para: Get the number of stored characters.
Get the width of the string in terminal cells.

simplesect.para: The sum of all character display widths.

briefdescription.para: Test if this string view is empty.
Access one character with bounds checking.

simplesect.para: A copy of the character at index.

briefdescription.para: Get an iterator to the first character.
Get an iterator past the last character.
Get a const iterator to the first character.
Get a const iterator past the last character.
Get a const reverse iterator to the last character.
Get a const reverse iterator past the first character.
Get a const reverse iterator to the last character.
Get a const reverse iterator past the first character.
Count the number of characters matching a fully styled character.

simplesect.para: The number of matching characters in this view.

briefdescription.para: Count the number of characters matching one code point regardless of style.

simplesect.para: The number of matching characters in this view.

briefdescription.para: Get the index of the next character with a given full style.

parameterdescription.para: The first local index to inspect.

simplesect.para: The local index of the next match, or npos.

briefdescription.para: Get the index of the next character matching one code point regardless of style.

parameterdescription.para: The first local index to inspect.

simplesect.para: The local index of the next match, or npos.

briefdescription.para: Get the index of the next matching character.

detaileddescription.para:
Ignores the character style. If startIndex is out of bounds, returns npos. If no character is found, returns npos. This function matches any character in the character set.
characterSet
The character-set to search for. Only compares single-code-point characters.
startIndex
The start index to search from. Defaults to 0.
The index of the next character or npos if not found.

briefdescription.para: Get the index of the next not matching character.

detaileddescription.para:
Ignores the character style. If startIndex is out of bounds, returns npos. If no character is found, returns npos. This function matches if no character in the character set matches the text.
characterSet
The character-set to search for. Only compares single-code-point characters.
startIndex
The start index to search from. Defaults to 0.
The index of the next character or npos if not found.

briefdescription.para: Get a sub-view.

detaileddescription.para:
If startIndex is out of bounds, returns an empty view. If length exceeds the view length, returns the remaining characters.
startIndex
The start index of the substring.
length
The number of characters after the start index.
The substring view or an empty view if startIndex is out of bounds.

briefdescription.para: Get a substring that fits into the given display width.

detaileddescription.para:
If a double-sized character is at the edge, it isn't included in the result. Therefore, the resulting string may be shorter than the display width.
displayWidth
The maximum width of the substring in display units.
alignment
The alignment of the cropped text. Only Alignment::Left and Alignment::Right are supported.
The cropped substring or an empty string if displayWidth is <=0.

briefdescription.para: Trim the given characters from the beginning and end of the view.

detaileddescription.para:
Only single-code-point characters are matched.
characters
The characters to remove from both ends. If empty, removes space, tab, and newline characters.
A trimmed string view.

briefdescription.para: Test if this string view contains control characters.

detaileddescription.para: As most control codes are filtered on construction, this mainly tests for NL and TAB.

briefdescription.para: Split the view into words at space, tab, carriage return, or newline characters.
Wrap this view into lines that have a maximum display width.

detaileddescription.para:
Paragraph breaks from newline characters are preserved using the selected paragraph spacing.
width
The maximum terminal width in cells. Must be greater than zero.
paragraphSpacing
The spacing to use between newline-separated paragraphs.
A sequence of materialized lines.

briefdescription.para: Count how many terminal lines this view occupies for a given terminal width.

parameterdescription.para: The available terminal width in cells. Must be greater than zero.

simplesect.para: The number of occupied terminal lines.

briefdescription.para: Get the natural rectangular size for this text view without wrapping.

detaileddescription.para:
The returned size is at least 1x1, preserves explicit non-trailing newline characters as separate lines, and uses terminal cell width for wide and combining characters.
The natural text size in terminal cells.

briefdescription.para: Calculate the height required to render this text view with WritableBuffer::drawText().

detaileddescription.para:
The given width is the full target rectangle width, including margins configured in options.
width
The available rectangle width in terminal cells.
options
The text options used for paragraph layout.
The required rectangle height in terminal cells.

briefdescription.para: Split this view into individual lines.

detaileddescription.para:
The string is split at the NL character that is not included in the result. Empty lines are preserved. A NL at the end of the view does not generate an additional empty line.
A sequence of line views.

briefdescription.para: Create a new string with the given style applied as a base.

parameterdescription.para: The style used as base for the resulting string.

simplesect.para: A new string with the base style applied.

briefdescription.para: A read-only view onto shared terminal string data.
